Article Title: Richard Petty reveals second ‘Intimidator’ in NASCAR, strongly resembling Dale Earnhardt’s Old-School Grit
Article Text:
Richard Petty reveals second ‘Intimidator’ in NASCAR, strongly resembling Dale Earnhardt’s Old-School Grit

Richard Petty reveals the Intimidator



There has been much discussion about the absence of superstar drivers in NASCAR, such as Dale Earnhardt or Jeff Gordon. Richard Petty may have a perspective on this issue. The king recently shared his thoughts on the topic. He picked the driver who ‘drives' like Dale Earnhardt, popularly known as The Intimidator. According to Petty, someone who is closer to Dale Earnhardt in terms of racing is Carson Hocevar.Recently, in a discussion with Dale Inman, Richard Petty mentioned the driver who resembles Dale Earnhardt on the track, Carson Hocevar of Spire Motorsports. Not in terms of appearance, but rather in the way he carries himself, shows old-school grit and races fiercely in every race in the NASCAR national series.“That boy in that 77, he has him in time. A lot of things he’s getting by with or doing are not really that wrong. If I go back and watch him run and stuff, he reminds me of Dale Earnhardt,” Petty said. “Earnhardt learned to get by with that stuff. That boy in the 77, he’s just going to have to learn to get by with the things that he’s doing.”But his co-host on the Richard Petty Family Racing podcast, Inman, had made a point. He believes that despite Hocevar's speed, he does not have a friend and is isolated.Inman said, “He’s got a lot of speed. He’s going to have to control himself a little bit better. But right now, he’s not making any friends, Richard. And I don’t know if he’ll overcome that enough to win a race.”However, Petty had the same response, and it was as Earnhardt said, “Earnhardt made no friends either.”At this statement, Inman further added, “I’m not sure they were alike. You say they aren’t. I don’t know. But even in a race at Atlanta, Richard, a lot of those cars were just on the ragged edge all day.”Whether he is the next Dale Earnhardt or not, Carson Hocevar, often known as "Hurricane Hocevar," has successfully proven himself. Despite the #77 Spire Motorsports driver having yet to claim a victory in 82 races so far, he has really gotten very close to winning.As Richard Petty feels that unreserved aggression, unapologetic actions, and lack of hesitancy place him in the same category as Dale Earnhardt was in his youth, Kyle Petty, the King’s son, does not believe differently.
Article Summary: Richard Petty reveals the Intimidator in NASCAR, strongly resembling Dale Earnhardt’s Old-School Grit. Carson Hocevar shows old-school grit and races fiercely in every race in the NASCAR national series. Despite the #77 Spire Motorsports driver having yet to claim a victory in 82 races so far, he has really gotten very close to winning.

Article Title: T20 World Cup: No fuss, no noise, just business - the New Zealand way
Article Text:
New Zealand's team huddles before the start of the T20 World Cup cricket match between Canada and New Zealand in Chennai, India, Tuesday, Feb. 17, 2026. (AP)

TimesofIndia.com in Colombo:

Why Pakistan don't trust Babar Azam any more | T20 World Cup 2026

Did they look bothered?



Playing at the same venue



Facing spin



Team news



New Zealand middle-order batter Mark Chapman looked perplexed when he was asked about the Super Eight groups, where all four table-toppers, who have done well in their respective groups, will end up facing each other. It has been done because of the pre-tournament seedings.The 31-year-old smiled and replied, “When it comes to scheduling and planning, that’s not really in my domain.”Chapman’s response sums up the way New Zealand play their cricket. No fuss, no theatrics, just straight down to business.After playing three matches in Chennai and one in Ahmedabad, all on red-soil pitches, the Kiwis had only one training session to adapt to the conditions ahead of their first Super Eight match against Pakistan at the R Premadasa Stadium.None of them rushed towards the pitch for a closer look. Once warm-ups were done, the players moved to the indoor nets. About an hour later, Daryl Mitchell came out barefoot to inspect the surface, spending a good fifteen minutes studying every inch of the 22 yards.Chapman, who did not get to see the surface, left it to the senior leadership group.“In the subcontinent, the toss always plays a factor depending on the conditions.I haven’t seen the pitch yet, so I don’t actually know what it looks like. There will be discussions behind the scenes with the senior leadership group about what we want to do,” he said.The black-soil pitches in Colombo do assist spinners, and with bigger boundaries and a slower outfield, it presents a new challenge for the 2021 T20 World Cup finalists.“We haven’t played in Sri Lanka yet. A lot of guys in our team have played a lot of cricket here, so I don’t see that playing too much of a factor. Guys understand what they need to do, and we are confident we will face whatever conditions are in front of us,” said Chapman, whose knees were heavily strapped.“In India, particularly on red-soil pitches, conditions have been favourable for batting. It has been tough for the bowlers, and the margins have been very small. We have seen scores close to 200 regularly.“Here, it is slightly different with the slower nature of the pitches. The bowlers will hopefully get a chance to show their skills, and spinners are more likely to play a part here,” he added.Although Pakistan are stationed in Sri Lanka, they played their group matches at two different venues in Colombo.New Zealand will be the only team playing all their Super Eight matches at the same venue, facing Pakistan on February 21, Sri Lanka on February 25, and England on February 27, all at the R Premadasa Stadium.“One of the challenges in tournaments like these is hopping from venue to venue and adapting. When we play three games at the same place, we learn with each game.“It is nice to turn up at the same venue with similar pitches and adjust. Tomorrow is our first game here, so we will have to adapt anyway, but we will understand the conditions better as we go,” he said.New Zealand’s first challenge will be Pakistan’s spin-heavy attack. Usman Tariq, Abrar Ahmad, Mohammad Nawaz, Saim Ayub, and Shadab Khan form a strong unit.“Pakistan have a number of very good spinners, each with their own threat. We have played them quite frequently over the last few years.“We are well aware of what they will bring. For us, it is about being really clear in how we want to play against them,” he said.Obviously, there was a Usman Tariq question: “Usman Tariq has a unique action with the way he pauses at the crease, so that is something to factor in."Chapman, whose role is to take on spinners in the middle overs, explained why running between the wickets will be crucial.“Given the slower pitch and bigger boundaries, you have to change your game plan. Against spinners, it is about different options like the sweep or reverse sweep. In India, you can target straight boundaries more.“For me, it is about finding different areas of the ground. There are big pockets here, so it is not all about sixes. Twos, fours, and running between the wickets are very important, especially if it is not a high-scoring game,” he said.New Zealand have suffered injury setbacks. Michael Bracewell has been ruled out of the remainder of the tournament after re-injuring his left calf. Captain Mitchell Santner missed the last group match against Canada due to illness, while Lockie Ferguson returned home for the birth of his first child.“Mitchell Santner looks pretty sprightly and excited to get back on the park, barring any last-minute illness.“Lockie has just returned and is coming off a long flight. We will see how he pulls up. I cannot confirm whether he will be in the playing eleven,” Chapman said.
Article Summary: New Zealand will play all their Super Eight matches at the same venue, facing Pakistan on February 21, Sri Lanka on February 25, and England on February 27. Black-soil pitches in Colombo do assist spinners, and with bigger boundaries and a slower outfield, it presents a new challenge for 2021 T20 World Cup finalists.

Article Title: Suryakumar Yadav’s Team India eyes big win vs Zimbabwe at Chepauk: No room for error
Article Text:
Captain Suryakumar Yadav, right, head coach Gautam Gambhir, second left, with the team. (PTI Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

India Playing XI drama: How three players are fighting for two spots | Sanju Samson IN?

CHENNAI: When was the last time Indian players have been forced to keep an eye on another game before taking the field? It’s difficult to remember but that’s exactly the position they find themselves in right now in the T20 World Cup. Before India take on Zimbabwe at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai on Thursday, Suryakumar Yadav & Co. will hope South Africa beat West Indies in Ahmedabad. If it goes the other way, it may well take things beyond their control, with Zimbabwe also needing to beat South Africa. While India’s resounding defeat against South Africa, which left them with a net run rate (NRR) of -3.8 has put them in this pickle, it wasn’t exactly a bolt from the blue.Right from the first game against the USA, the Indian batting lineup was found wanting, but there seemed to be a strange reluctance to accept the shortcomings. Captain Suryakumar Yadav virtually laughed off a journalist’s query about probable changes in the batting order before the South Africa game but the humbling loss to them has changed every equation. Changes are now being mulled and there’s a strong possibility that Sanju Samson will be drafted in to open the batting.“There can be changes, yes. There are three left-handers up top and we have lost a wicket in the first over in the last three games. I personally don’t think that there is much problem, but we will see how it can be dealt with,” batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said on Wednesday.Wednesday was a hot day in Chennai, with summer setting in, and only two players — C Varun and Tilak Varma — turned up for the optional training session. On Tuesday evening, though, the team had a four-hour session, where every batter looked to go for their shots right from the word go. Surya and Tilak, who may well be coming one slot down if Sanju plays, looked the most intent on hitting every ball out of the park. Both have struggled with their strike rate and they know that at No. 4 and 5, there won’t be too much time to settle in.Kotak, though, wasn’t ready to buy the idea that the duo have been struggling for form. “The South Africa game was really bad but that’s done. But against Pakistan we knew what we wanted and 175 was above par. Tilak and Surya were playing according to the situation,” the batting coach said.While regaining batting momentum is prime on India’s agenda, there’s also a strong likelihood that Axar Patel will be back in place of Rinku Singh, who has gone home to look after his ailing father.“At No. 8, an allrounder is what we are probably looking at,” Kotak said, in an answer to a different query. But it left enough indication that Axar, whose exclusion in Ahmedabad raised eyebrows, will be back.The Chepauk pitch is likely to be a belter — a 200-plus score shouldn’t be a problem if India bat first and don’t lose wickets in a heap. That can put Zimbabwe, a competent side without much firepower, completely out of the game. Ryan Burt, one of Zimbabwe’s top allrounders, feels India will also carry excess baggage. “It’s as much a must-win game for us as it is for India. They lost pretty badly in the first game and so did we. There were, of course, learnings, and we want to take that forward,” Burt said.The Zimbabwe team had travelled on Tuesday and Wednesday’s training session was their first at the Chepauk. The conditions at the venue are quite different to what they have experienced in Sri Lanka, where they beat Australia and Sri Lanka. Burt agreed that’s going to be a challenge.There was one piece of good news for Zimbabwe, with their captain Sikandar Raza — who was struck in his bowling hand while playing against West Indies — regaining fitness.
Article Summary: India take on Zimbabwe at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai on Thursday. Suryakumar Yadav & Co. will hope South Africa beat West Indies in Ahmedabad. If it goes the other way, it may well take things beyond their control, with Zimbabwe also needing to beat South Africa.

Article Title: NHL season resumes as Florida Panthers and Buffalo Sabres intensify Eastern Conference playoff race
Article Text:
The Florida Panthers celebrate the win after game 7 of the Eastern Conference Playoffs (Credit: Getty Image)

Paul Maurice: Florida Panthers Back at Practice, Talks Finland Olympic Coaching Rumor

Florida Panthers and Buffalo Sabres playoff race for the Eastern Conference



The NHL is fully back into action since the Olympic break, and the competition towards the Stanley Cup Playoffs is narrowing in the Eastern Conference. It is only a few weeks until the regular season ends, and thus, competitors and aspirants are fighting off every point that they can get in what is likely to be an exciting stretch run full of decisive NHL games and playoff consequences.Some of the teams in the spotlight include the Florida Panthers and the Buffalo Sabres, both of which are striving to cement their positions in the postseason. The league has come back to play, and talks have resurfaced in the NHL news wraps. Fans are following the injury reports, trade talks and NHL match previews, which may influence the Eastern Conference standings.The Florida Panthers start their stretch run in a bad mood following the ACL surgery of the captain, Aleksander Barkov, who was injured at the beginning of the season. It has led Florida to rearrange its leadership core since he was absent, but the timely uplift was given because Matthew Tkachuk returned before the break. Having approximately 25 games remaining, Florida needs to have consecutive performances to reduce the number of points in the Atlantic Division.In the meantime, the Buffalo Sabres are trying to break a decade-long franchise playoff drought. A midseason change of front office brought a new momentum, and the Sabres shot briefly into a wild-card position in an impressive winning streak.Nevertheless, the recent results of NHL matches indicate that there can be no room to be wasted, and more difficult matches with the best opponents are on the way.The two clubs are also keeping track of NHL trade rumors before the March deadline because they are aware that a strategic acquisition can boost the balance. In the case of Florida, it is the issue of defensive depth, whereas Buffalo could be interested in having the veterans to help its young base in the time of the high-pressure games.When the NHL season restarts, every interdivisional and inter-team match will be even more meaningful.
Article Summary: The NHL is fully back into action since the Olympic break, and the competition towards the Stanley Cup Playoffs is narrowing in the Eastern Conference. The Florida Panthers and the Buffalo Sabres are both striving to cement their positions in the postseason. The two clubs are also keeping track of NHL trade rumors before the March deadline.

Article Title: Jack Eichel ruled out as Vegas Golden Knights face Los Angeles Kings in NHL return
Article Text:
Vegas Golden Knights have begun contract extension negotiations with Jack Eichel (Imagn Images)

Jack Eichel on Team USA winning gold in men's hockey

Jack Eichel Vegas Golden Knights injury update for game against the Los Angeles Kings



The Vegas Golden Knights enter the NHL again on Wednesday morning without a significant part of their team. The team has already confirmed that its star player in the center, Jack Eichel, will not play the Los Angeles Kings on the road. The update is made because the Golden Knights begin their regular season after the international break.The fact that Eichel was absent is remarkable considering that he was a top-line center and an offensive driver with Vegas. As the playoff placement becomes tighter in the Western Conference, the Golden Knights will have to make a timely change against a Kings team that has been very effective on the home front. The preview of this NHL match focuses on how Vegas will cope without its star playmaker.On Tuesday, the Vegas Golden Knights announced that Jack Eichel has been excluded from the match with the Los Angeles Kings. The team did not show a long-term concern but assured that he would not be available for this NHL match today.Eichel has recently attended the international tournament break representing his country and has since gone back to team activities. But the Golden Knights are playing on the side of caution since the second half of the season commences.The workload and recovery seem to be one of the primary issues to address, particularly as there are critical games forthcoming.Jack Eichel has been the core of the Golden Knights' offensive framework through the season, with crucial goals and assists in the stressful moments. His absence will probably increase the responsibilities on the second and third lines, with Vegas supposed to mix up combinations before the puck drop.As an NHL news roundup, the development makes the Pacific Division race interesting. The Los Angeles Kings, on the other hand, will be going to the match to exploit a somewhat weaker Vegas lineup.More NHL news on the condition of Jack Eichel will likely be received in the next few days as the Golden Knights move on their road trip.
Article Summary: Jack Eichel has been excluded from the match with the Los Angeles Kings. The team did not show a long-term concern but assured that he would not be available for this NHL match today. Vegas Golden Knights begin their regular season after the international break. The development makes the Pacific Division race interesting.

Article Title: Bryce Young: Jaylen Waddle revisits Bryce Young’s Alabama campus visit with lighthearted comment
Article Text:
Jaylen Waddle (Image via: IG/X)

Jaylen Waddle jokingly asks Bryce Young to share his Heisman after recalling Alabama visit

From a simple Alabama visit to two first round NFL draft picks

Bryce Young’s 2021 season at Alabama remains one of the most impressive performances by a college quarterback in recent memory. He completed 366 of 547 passes for 4,872 yards and 47 touchdowns, guiding Alabama all the way to the College Football Playoff national championship game. His steady leadership and ability to deliver in big moments earned him the Heisman Trophy.But years later, one of his former teammates believes he deserves a small piece of that famous trophy. Miami Dolphins wide receiver Jaylen Waddle recently joked that he should receive half of Young’s Heisman because he helped convince him to choose Alabama during his campus visit. The comments were playful, yet they offered a fun behind the scenes look at how one relaxed trip helped shape the future of the Crimson Tide.Before Bryce Young became Alabama’s starting quarterback, he was committed to USC. During his visit to Alabama, Jaylen Waddle served as his host. Speaking on “The Set” podcast, Waddle shared how simple and laid back the visit really was.“I got to take some credit because I had Bryce Young as a recruit,” Waddle said during an appearance on the most recent episode of “The Set” podcast. “He was committed to USC. …“I had him as a recruit. He switched to Alabama. I feel like I need half of that Heisman from Alabama.I feel like I need half of the Heisman. Not the whole thing. Just send me half of it. That’s all I ask Alabama to do. That’s my recruit, bro. That’s my recruit. I switched him.”Waddle explained that Young mostly wanted to relax and play video games during the visit rather than follow a strict schedule. “Bryce ain’t want to do nothing but play (NBA) 2K,” Waddle said. “He played 2K against Pat Surtain. Pat was my roommate. Bryce from Cali, bro. He don’t want to do nothing: ‘I want to play 2K.’“We scooped him from what’s it called? Me, him and (Alabama offensive coordinator Steve Sarkisian) chop it up, this, that and the third. Take bro back with us. He just want to play 2K. Play 2K one, two hours. Back to where we got him. I think he ate or something like that. Went to eat and played 2K and just chilling, just vibing. Good vibes because ain’t nothing to do in Alabama. Nothing to do. …"“Send half of it. That’s all I ask. Send half of it. Send half of it, Bryce. Come on, I’m telling you. Send half of it, bro.”Young eventually changed his commitment and joined Alabama, where he developed into a Heisman winner and one of the top quarterbacks in college football. Waddle had already moved on to the NFL after the 2020 season, becoming the sixth overall pick in the 2021 draft. Both players now compete at the highest level, but their connection goes back to a simple campus visit filled with video games and easy conversations.
Article Summary: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Jaylen Waddle helped convince Bryce Young to choose Alabama. Young was committed to USC, but Waddle convinced him to visit the Crimson Tide. Waddle said Young mostly wanted to relax and play video games during the visit. Young eventually joined Alabama, where he developed into a Heisman winner.

Article Title: Anthony Joshua’s driver, Adeniyi Mobolaji Kayode in crash case appears in Nigerian court as hearing is further adjourned till March
Article Text:
Anthony Joshua driver faces four charges as new court details emerge in deadly Nigeria road accident (Image via Getty)

Anthony Joshua Stays in Nigeria After Deadly Crash | What Happens Next?

Charges filed against driver after fatal expressway collision



The driver involved in the car crash that injured British boxing star Anthony Joshua and killed two of his close team members has appeared before a Nigerian court. The accident happened on December 29 near Lagos. Joshua’s personal trainer, Latif Ayodele, and strength coach, Sina Ghami, died at the scene after their vehicle collided with a stationary lorry. The former heavyweight champion, 36, survived and was taken to hospital with injuries.Adeniyi Mobolaji Kayode visited the Sagamu Magistrates Court in Ogun State on Wednesday. His case was adjourned until March 13 as prosecutors requested more time to gather and prepare evidence. Authorities have filed several charges against him. They include causing death by dangerous driving and operating a vehicle without a valid driver’s licence. The crash occurred on the busy Lagos-Ibadan Expressway in south-west Nigeria.Adeniyi Mobolaji Kayode was driving a Lexus SUV carrying Anthony Joshua and members of his team when the vehicle struck a parked truck. Investigators later reported that the car was travelling at a high speed before impact.The collision left Joshua with minor injuries but resulted in the deaths of Latif Ayodele and Sina Ghami.The defendant has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving, reckless and negligent driving, driving without due care, and driving without a valid national driver’s licence. He made his second appearance in court but has not yet entered a plea. Prosecutors explained to the magistrate that they required additional time to complete the evidence file before proceedings could continue.The driver had earlier been charged in January and was granted bail set at five million naira. The court allowed him temporary release while the investigation continued. Officials said the case involves multiple technical reports and witness statements connected to the crash.The incident has also highlighted concerns about road safety on Nigerian highways. The Lagos-Ibadan Expressway is one of the country’s busiest roads and has recorded several serious accidents in recent years. Authorities are expected to continue the case in March, when the court will review the gathered evidence and decide the next legal steps.Joshua was treated in hospital and discharged two days after the accident. He later travelled back to the United Kingdom to recover. The deaths of his trainer and strength coach deeply affected his team.
Article Summary: Adeniyi Mobolaji Kayode visited the Sagamu Magistrates Court in Ogun State on Wednesday. His case was adjourned until March 13 as prosecutors requested more time to gather and prepare evidence. Authorities have filed several charges against him. They include causing death by dangerous driving and operating a vehicle without a valid driver’s licence.

Article Title: 'It was just too much': Sanjay Manjrekar tears into Gautam Gambhir's tactics
Article Text:
India's head coach Gautam Gambhir and skipper Suryakumar Yadav (ANI Photo)

Is this the end of the T20 World Cup for India? Greenstone Lobo predicts results

Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar has taken a swipe at head coach Gautam Gambhir following India’s crushing 76-run defeat to South Africa national cricket team in their Super 8 opener of the T20 World Cup in Ahmedabad.The loss, the heaviest ever for India in T20 World Cup history, has pushed the defending champions to the edge of elimination and severely dented their net run rate. Widely backed to become the first side to successfully defend a T20 World Cup crown, the Suryakumar Yadav-led outfit now face must-win games to stay alive in the tournament.Manjrekar questioned several tactical decisions, particularly the elevation of Washington Sundar to No. 5 during India’s unsuccessful chase of 188 after early wickets. The move came after Sundar was preferred over vice-captain Axar Patel, a selection that had already sparked debate, though team management described it as a tactical call.Speaking on Stump Mic on CNN-News18, Manjrekar used a pointed analogy to describe Sundar’s expanding role in the XI."Kailas Jeevan, there used to be a medicine by that name back in the day. If you had a headache, you would use it. If you had a stomach issue, you would take it.It was an all-in-one remedy. Gautam Gambhir seems to have an all-in-one solution for everything too, Washington Sundar," Manjrekar said on CNN-News18's Stump Mic while analysing India's batting performance."If there's a batting problem, Washi. If there's a bowling problem, Washi. He was promoted up the order to No. 5 today, and then Rinku Singh and Hardik Pandya walked in at No. 7. It was just too much," he added.South Africa capitalised on India’s vulnerability against changes of pace, with Lungi Ngidi troubling the batters through well-disguised slower deliveries. Manjrekar stressed that this area requires urgent attention, singling out captain Suryakumar Yadav ."Forget fast bowling, focus on playing the slower balls. They are not able to handle slower balls or spinners. The captain is struggling the most against slower deliveries," he said.India now turn their focus to a decisive clash against Zimbabwe national cricket team. Not only must they win to keep their semi-final hopes intact, but they will also need a comprehensive victory to repair the damage inflicted on their net run rate in Ahmedabad.
Article Summary: Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar takes a swipe at head coach Gautam Gambhir. India lost 76-run to South Africa in their Super 8 opener of the T20 World Cup in Ahmedabad. The loss, the heaviest ever for India, has pushed them to the edge of elimination and severely dented their net run rate.

Article Title: Carl Froch rules out Conor Benn’s rematch with Ryan Garcia, says the Zuffa Boxing new hiring will be “outclassed”
Article Text:
Conor Benn (Image Via Getty)

Carl Froch questions Conor Benn’s readiness but calls the split “just business”



“He’d get SPLATTERED!” Carl Froch doubts Conor Benn vs Ryan Garcia | Split Decision

Carl Froch believes a future fight between Conor Benn and Ryan Garcia would be very one-sided. The former world champion watched Garcia dominate Mario Barrios in Las Vegas and said the American looked far ahead of Benn’s current level. Garcia dropped Barrios in just 30 seconds and then controlled the full twelve rounds to win the WBC welterweight world title. Benn, who is ranked No.1 contender for that belt, was ringside for the bout.The potential fight now looks uncertain after Benn left long-time promoter Eddie Hearn and Matchroom Boxing. The British boxer has signed a lucrative deal with Dana White’s Zuffa Boxing. The decision shocked the boxing world and upset Hearn, who supported Benn through his doping case in 2022.Carl Froch said he simply does not see Conor Benn competing with Ryan Garcia at elite level. He explained that, in his opinion, Benn has not yet beaten top opponents to prove he belongs in a world-title fight of that size. Speaking to The Sun, Froch noted that Garcia would outbox him easily and move around him throughout the contest. He added that Benn sometimes dislikes hearing this criticism, but the results on his record still leave doubts about his readiness.He pointed out that Benn’s biggest name opponent was Chris Eubank Jr. Froch argued that Eubank was no longer at his peak and had already taken heavy defeats in recent years. Because of that, Froch believes Benn has not yet done enough to justify fighting a newly crowned champion like Garcia.Benn has also not competed at welterweight for nearly four years. His recent fights were at middleweight against Eubank. He lost the first fight in April but clearly won the November rematch. Before that, his career stalled when he tested positive for clomiphene in 2022. Benn denied wrongdoing and fought the charges with Hearn’s backing. Eventually the case was dropped and his suspension lifted.After returning, Benn reportedly earned close to $20 million from the two Eubank bouts. Soon after, he agreed to a one-fight deal worth more than $10 million with Zuffa Boxing. Hearn felt hurt by the decision and insiders described it as a major betrayal, especially because communication was handled through lawyers rather than directly.Despite this, Froch defended Benn’s choice. He said boxing careers can end suddenly through injury and fighters must secure the biggest paydays while they can.
Article Summary: Carl Froch says Conor Benn is not ready for a world title fight with Ryan Garcia. Froch believes Benn has not yet beaten top opponents to prove he belongs in a world-title fight of that size. Benn left long-time promoter Eddie Hearn and Matchroom Boxing to sign a lucrative deal with Dana White's Zuffa Boxing.

Article Title: Lionel Messi opens up about the special Barcelona night he proposed to Antonela Roccuzzo on Miro de Atrás podcast
Article Text:
Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo (Image via: Alexander Scheuber/Getty)

Inside the romantic Barcelona night Lionel Messi proposed

How Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo made their bond official

When Lionel Messi talks about his personal life, fans listen closely. The Inter Miami star recently appeared on the Miro de Atrás podcast with Argentine goalkeeper Nahuel Guzmán. During the conversation, he spoke about football, Argentina, and different moments from his long career. But one memory stood out. Messi shared how he asked Antonela Roccuzzo to marry him.The story goes back to his time in Barcelona. By then, Messi and Antonela had already been together for many years. They were raising two sons, Thiago and Mateo. Marriage was not a sudden idea. It felt like the natural next step in a relationship that was already strong.Messi said the proposal happened during a simple evening in Barcelona while he was playing for FC Barcelona. There was no big crowd. No public announcement. Just a quiet night together.“We had already been together for many years. We already had Thiago and Mateo; we already had two sons. And well, one time we went out to eat in Barcelona, at a hotel, we spent the night, and that's where I proposed to her-but more or less it was already something that was known, it had to be, but it was more in a romantic style.It wasn't like the chain suddenly broke one day and you said, 'Ah, let's get married, that's it,'” Messi shared.His words showed that the decision was calm and thoughtful. It was not rushed. It was something both of them understood would happen.Messi also explained that the proposal was about making their long relationship official. “No, no, it was more romantic, but more or less like that-setting the date, something like that. Yes, because it was already like the bond was being made official,” he said.The couple later married in Rosario, Argentina. The wedding drew attention from around the world. Today, they have three children and are building their life in the United States, where Messi plays for Inter Miami CF.Through every move and every season, Antonela remained part of his everyday life. From their early days in Rosario to a quiet night in Barcelona and now a new chapter in Miami, their story continues to grow. shaped by family life, shared memories, and the everyday moments they build together.
Article Summary: Lionel Messi recently appeared on the Miro de Atrás podcast with Argentine goalkeeper Nahuel Guzmán. Messi shared how he asked Antonela Roccuzzo to marry him. The couple later married in Rosario, Argentina. Today, they have three children and are building their life in the United States.

Article Title: Michael van Gerwen gives health update and confirms return for Belfast Premier League Darts night
Article Text:
Michael van Gerwen (via Getty Images)

Michael Van Gerwen set to face Gerwyn Price on Belfast Premier League Darts comeback night



Michael van Gerwen has recently provided a major and positive health update before the next night of the Premier League Darts. Previously, the Dutch star had to withdraw from the Glasgow event last week because of his illness. It was a worrying period for his fans as the three-time world champion also missed other tournaments. His absence meant he could not compete in Players Championship 5 and 6. Now, the 36-year-old has confirmed that he is recovering and plans to return to action in Belfast Premier League Darts.The former world No.1 darts performer did not publicly reveal the exact illness he was suffering from. However, his close friend and fellow darts player Vincent van der Voort has revealed that he had been taking antibiotics. Van Gerwen admitted he was seriously unwell but is now improving. He is expected to play on Thursday, February 26, in Northern Ireland. His return is important from tournament perspective because the Premier League season is still in its early stages, and points are crucial for qualification.Michael Van Gerwen said he is ready to travel and compete again. Speaking to AD.nl , he said, “I’m feeling much better, but I was really poorly”.His recent absence has affected his rhythm. The Dutchman has previously missed four tournaments in a row. His latest missed events were Players Championship 5 and 6. Before falling sick, he had struggled for form in the Premier League Darts. In night two of the competition, Welsh star Gerwyn Price defeated him comfortably. Price beat Van Gerwen 6-0 in the quarter-finals of the second event and later won the night with a 6-3 victory in the final.Now the two rivals will meet once again. They are scheduled to face each other in the first round in Belfast on Thursday. It will be a difficult test for Michael Van Gerwen as he immediately returns after illness. Still, Van Gerwen’s presence is a big boost for the tournament because he remains one of the sport’s most popular and successful players.The Premier League format means every match matters, and early defeats can hurt a player’s chances of reaching the play-offs. Even so, Van Gerwen’s experience could help him quickly regain momentum if his fitness holds up during the night.
Article Summary: Michael van Gerwen set to face Gerwyn Price on Belfast Premier League Darts comeback night on Thursday. The Dutch star had to withdraw from the Glasgow event last week because of his illness. His absence meant he could not compete in Players Championship 5 and 6.

Article Title: Skateboarding World Championships 2025: Sao Paulo event from 4-8 March with Olympic champions, full schedule, participants and more
Article Text:
Yuto Horigome (via Getty)

Olympic champions headline strong pre-seed list



The Skateboarding World Championships São Paulo 2025 for park and street will begin on 4 March in Brazil. The five-day competition will run until 8 March and marks the first stop of the World Skateboarding Tour in 2026. The event was earlier planned for Washington D.C. in September 2025 but was later moved. It will also be the first major world championship since the World Cup Kitakyushu Street event held in Japan in November last year.Many of the world’s best skateboarders are already confirmed. The four Olympic champions from the Paris 2024 Games will compete. They include Arisa Trew in women’s park, Yoshizawa Coco in women’s street, Keegan Palmer in men’s park and Yuto Horigome in men’s street. In each discipline, 32 qualifiers will join eight pre-seeded skaters. All of them will compete for the four world titles in São Paulo.The pre-seeded skaters will go straight into the quarter-final stage. In the women’s park event, Olympic gold medallist Arisa Trew of Australia leads the list. She will be joined by Spain’s Naia Laso and several strong Japanese riders including Mizuho Hasegawa, Hinano Kusaki, Cocona Hiraki and Sakura Yosozumi.Brazil’s Raicca Ventura and Japan’s Mei Sugawara are also among the top seeds.The men’s park field also looks competitive. Brazil’s Augusto Akio and Pedro Barros are key home hopes. Olympic champion Keegan Palmer of Australia is another major contender. Other pre-seeds include Viktor Solmunde, Tate Carew, Egoitz Bijueska, Yuro Nagahara and Gui Khury.In women’s street, Japan dominates the seedings. Oda Yumeka, Yoshizawa Coco, Nakayama Funa and Momiji Nishiya are all in the top eight. Australia’s Chloe Covell and Brazil’s Rayssa Leal will draw strong fan support. Liz Akama and Ibuki Matsumoto complete the list.The men’s street competition features several top Japanese riders. Shirai Sora, Sasaki Toa, Netsuke Kairi, Onodera Ginwoo, Yukito Aoki and Olympic champion Yuto Horigome are seeded. Argentina’s Matias Dell Olio and Colombia’s J.C. Gonzalez Ortiz are also included.Practice sessions will take place from 1 to 3 March. Official competition begins on 4 March with qualifiers. Quarter-finals, semi-finals and finals will follow across the next four days.Fans can watch selected live coverage on the Olympic Channel through Olympics.com, although some regional viewing limits may apply. The championship is expected to be an important early event in the new Olympic cycle and a key ranking opportunity for skateboarders around the world.
Article Summary: Skateboarding World Championships São Paulo 2025 for park and street will begin on 4 March in Brazil. The five-day competition will run until 8 March and marks the first stop of the World Skateboarding Tour in 2026. It will also be the first major world championship since the World Cup Kitakyushu Street event held in Japan in November last year.

Article Title: Montreal Canadiens forward Patrik Laine’s $8.7 million cap hit less daunting ahead of NHL trade deadline
Article Text:
Patrik Laine #92 of the Montreal Canadiens looks on against the Washington Capitals (Credit: Getty Image)

A door closes.

Montreal Canadiens’ Patrik Laine trade outlook before March 6 deadline

With a few days to the trade deadline in the NHL, set to be on March 6, the Montreal Canadiens face several unanswered roster questions, and Patrik Laine is among the most talked about topics in recent NHL trade rumors. The veteran winger has not been able to play most of the season due to undergoing core muscle surgery and, therefore, has not been that influential in the match outcomes in the NHL. His uncertain status continues to shape front office discussions leaguewide.On the face of it, Laine has a huge cap hit of 8.7 million that can serve as a significant obstacle to any player who is venturing into a deal. League executives are not so worried about the situation, however, as the raw number would suggest. The financial problem can not be as limiting as taken into account in the current NHL updates since the contract has expired after this season, and Montreal retains its options in the form of salary retention.Patrik Laine has not been playing a game in the NHL with Montreal since October, but the fact that he resumed practicing during the Olympic break was a significant boost. From an NHL injury updates viewpoint, the teams will be keen on whether he can record game action before the deadline.Even a short-term recovery would alleviate the anxiety around conditioning and endurance.Laine is one of the more dangerous pure shooters in the league when he is healthy. His prowess on the power play and creating on the rush is a quality that makes him appealing to playoff-bound teams that are seeking finishing talent. Since he has one more year left of his deal, the process of acquiring clubs would be only of short term financial risk.The second significant consideration is the vacant salary retention positions in Montreal. The Canadiens can keep a serviceable amount of the cap hit of Laine, reducing the load of a competitor at the cap floor. Such flexibility expands the market and gives Montreal a stronger bargaining power.The result is that the seriousness of trade talks will depend on the health of Laine. In case he comes back and makes a positive contribution towards future NHL match outcomes, curiosity might develop rapidly before March 6.
Article Summary: Patrik Laine is among the most talked about topics in recent NHL trade rumors. The veteran winger has not been able to play most of the season due to undergoing core muscle surgery. Laine has a huge cap hit of 8.7 million that can serve as a significant obstacle to any player.

Article Title: Anthony Davis Trae Young Dynamic Duo: After Mavs letdown, Anthony Davis eyes dominant new era with Trae Young in Washington
Article Text:
Anthony Davis thinks he and Trae Young can become a dynamic duo (Images via Getty)

Anthony Davis thinks he and Trae Young will make a dynamic duo

Building a defensive identity around Anthony Davis

The Washington Wizards’ aggressive mid-season roster overhaul has shifted the gears in the nation’s capital as the team looks to build a contender worthy roster. It started with a series of blockbuster trades that landed All-Stars Anthony Davis and Trae Young at Washington indicating that the franchise is pivoting toward a high quality offensive identity.Even though both the players are currently sidelined with injuries, Anthony Davis has already begun visualizing the synergy and partnership with teammate Trae Young. These trades were a gamble for Wizards who currently positioned in the bottom three of the Eastern Conference with a 16-41 record. But the expected statistical prowess with the Davis-Young pairs offers a blueprint for Wizards’ mindset in the 2026-27 season.In a latest interview with Monumental Sports Network , Anthony Davis sat down with Wes Hall and talked about his trade to Wizards, future prospects and synergy between him and his new teammates.About his pairing with Trae Young, Davis said, "I think me and Trae can be a dynamic duo... all of these young guys can score the basketball. His passing ability, his ability to shoot the basketball, ability to get in the paint. I feel like any time I've had a point guard that can throw lobs, who can make the right reads and read the game like Trae, I've been great.Everything he brings to the game is unreal."Davis also commented about his collaboration with Sarr on the hardwood and said, "It's going to be insane."The hype around Davis-Trae pairing stems from their elite playmaking skills at the post and around the perimeter. Before the injury, Trae Young was averaging 19.3 points and 8.9 assists per game with a 41.5% shooting efficiency from the floor for the Hawks. His ability to break through defenses and bring out the A game is expected to become unstoppable with Anthony Davis, who also recorded 20.4 points and 11.1 rebounds per game in 20 appearances this season.While with Trae Young, who is currently recovering from a sprained right knee, the offensive potential is undeniable, Wizards are also equally focused on the defensive dominance that Anthony Davis brings in. Standing at 6'10" with a wingspan of 7'5.5", Davis is set to mentor rookie sensation Alex Sarr, who’s making splashes in the league with 2.0 blocks per game.With Davis expected to sit out the remainder of the 2025-26 campaign due to the left-hand injury, the Washington Wizards will prioritize long-term stability over immediate results. They are aiming to debut their healthy dynamic duo at the start of next season.
Article Summary: Anthony Davis thinks he and Trae Young will make a dynamic duo. Davis is set to mentor rookie sensation Alex Sarr, who’s making splashes in the league with 2.0 blocks per game. The Washington Wizards are aiming to debut their healthy dynamic duo at the start of next season.

Article Title: Chicago Bears head coach Ben Johnson makes his longstanding feud with Green Bay Packers HC Matt LaFleur known
Article Text:
Chicago Bears head coach Ben Johnson makes his longstanding feud with Green Bay Packers HC Matt LaFleur known (Image via Getty: Matt LaFleur and Ben Johnson)

Bears coach Ben Johnson admits to never talking to Packers coach Matt LaFleur

Ben Johnson and Matt LaFleur rivalry

Historically, the Green Bay Packers have won 109 games against the Chicago Bears in 213 games. Wait, before one does the math, leaving just 98 wins for Ben Johnson ’s Bears. Has he kept this in his mind for far too long? Why else would he reignite a past feud with Packers head coach Matt LaFleur?When NBC Sports’ Mike Florio asked Johnson at the NFL Combine about the Packers, the Chicago coach said, “Who likes the Packers? The Bears and the Packers should not like each other. It’s as simple as that. That’s going to make this rivalry, this game something that people are going to watch here going forward.”Florio asked Johnson whether he had spoken to LaFleur at all or had any relationship with him. That’s when Johnson said, “No, we don’t talk.”Now comes the blunt touch to his comment: When Florio asked him if Johnson wanted it to stay that way, where he does not talk to LaFleur, Johnson replied without a second thought or a blink, “I’m good with it.”The analyst pressed if the Packers coach had tried to reach out, but Johnson admitted, “No.” It appears the feeling’s mutual.Johnson had just tasted the first few days of a head coach’s role in Chicago, after a five-year stint with the Detroit Lions. In his introductory speech, he mentioned that he enjoyed beating LaFleur twice a year as the Lions’ offensive coordinator. This was the first observation.Then, a little more fire came when their postgame handshake went viral online.After the Bears defeated the Packers in the 2025 season’s NFC Wild Card Round, Johnson had a brief, quick, subtle, icy-cold, and momentary handshake with LaFleur.When LaFleur came to him, waiting to exchange a few words and say congratulations, Johnson did not wait for him to say anything! He came running, shook hands with LaFleur, and went off running in the other direction.What follows a month later, before the 2026 season? “We don’t talk,” and LaFleur has not reached out to talk either. If someone greets a losing team’s coach as Johnson did, it may have stayed with him.Plus, the aftermath that came after Chicago had won the wild card? It was even wilder than one can imagine. We know, digging old graves, but that’s what’s fueled this feud!So, after the game, as a head coach must deliver his winning speech for his winning team, Johnson says, “F**k the Packers! F**k them! I f*****g hate those guys!” No wonder Green Bay’s coach has not tried to talk to this coach.
Article Summary: Bears coach Ben Johnson admits to never talking to Packers coach Matt LaFleur. After the Bears defeated the Packers in the 2025 season’s NFC Wild Card Round, Johnson said, “F**k the Packers! F**k them! I f*****g hate those guys!”

Article Title: The Traitors Season 4 Twist: “Her being a traitor irked Me”: Kylie Kelce shares honest reaction to Mama Kelce on The Traitors

Unlike the standard villains of the show, Donna’s identity was hidden even from her fellow Traitors, leaving her to navigate the game in complete isolation.Speaking with host Beau Allen, Kylie Kelce didn’t hold back her frustration with how the show’s producers handled "Mama Kelce." Despite Donna being eliminated after just two-and-a-half episodes, the fallout is still a hot topic in the Kelce household.Kylie admitted during the podcast.Kylie argued that the twist actually put Donna at a disadvantage, preventing her from forming the "sneaky" alliances that typically help Traitors survive.Kylie added.While Kylie expressed her frustration with the game's mechanics, she remained supportive of Donna’s decision to keep the family in the dark while filming. Article Title: Alex Ovechkin: Alex Ovechkin One Goal from Passing Gordie Howe for Second Most Home Goals in NHL
Article Text:
Alex Ovechkin with the Washington Capitals (Via Getty Images)

The Washington Capitals and Alex Ovechkin remain barely out of a playoff position

Alex Ovechkin returns to Capital One Arena on Wednesday night, chasing another milestone in his legendary career. The Washington captain needs one goal at home to pass Gordie Howe for second place on the NHL's all-time home goals list.Ovechkin spent the Olympic break relaxing with friends and family in Dubai after Russia was banned from competing in the Milano Cortina 2026 Olympics. Now refreshed and ready, the 40-year-old faces the Philadelphia Flyers in a crucial Metropolitan Division showdown at 7 p.m. ET.The Capitals sit outside playoff position at 29-23-7 with 65 points. Washington trails the Boston Bruins by four points for the second wild card from the East and sits four points behind the New York Islanders for third in the Metropolitan Division.Ovechkin knows the margin for error has vanished. The Capitals face a difficult path to the postseason with 26 games remaining in the regular season. "We're going to have to win almost every game to make the playoffs," Ovechkin told NHL.com. "We're still in a battle."The Russian superstar continues to defy Father Time with 48 points through 59 games this season, including 22 goals and 26 assists. For someone who turned 40 just a couple of months ago, that pace has been dizzying.But so far, Ovi is pulling his weight in his 21st NHL season.Ovechkin currently has 448 home goals at Capital One Arena, tied with Howe for second place in NHL history. Wayne Gretzky holds the record with 492 home goals scored across his legendary career. Philadelphia arrives in Washington on a 3-4-3 slide over their last 10 games.The Flyers sit at 25-20-11 with 61 points, four points behind the Capitals in the Metropolitan Division standings. The struggling form has raised questions about whether Philadelphia will pivot to selling at the NHL Trade Deadline on March 6 at 3 p.m. ET.Several pending free agents could become available if the Flyers fall further out of playoff contention. Ovechkin already passed Gretzky for the all-time NHL career goals record in April 2025, scoring his 895th goal against the New York Islanders.But while Ovi has been putting up the numbers, the Capitals have been coming up short in several key games. Tonight’s game can be the turning point for Washington as it looks to push past the Flyers. The race for the final playoff spot remains as tight as ever, and if Ovechkin wants to make the postseason once again, tonight's game is a must-win.
Article Summary: Alex Ovechkin needs one goal at home to pass Gordie Howe for second place on the NHL's all-time home goals list. The 40-year-old faces the Philadelphia Flyers in a crucial Metropolitan Division showdown at 7 p.m. ET.

Article Title: Olympic gold queens Chloe Kim and Suni Lee reunite in New York City for a star-studded champion meetup
Article Text:
Chloe Kim and Suni Lee (Image Via Getty)

Chloe Kim and Sunisa “Suni” Lee share Olympic bond during New York meet-up

Just days after the 2026 Winter Olympics ended, Chloe Kim and Suni Lee gave fans a moment they did not expect. The two Olympic champions met in New York City and shared a photo together, and social media quickly lit up. Chloe Kim had just finished competing at the Milan Cortina Winter Games, where she won a silver medal in women’s halfpipe snowboarding.That medal marked her third Olympic medal overall. While the pressure of competition was behind her, she was back in the United States doing media appearances when she met up with Suni Lee. The photo showed two of Team USA’s biggest stars smiling side by side. Between them, they now hold nine Olympic medals. For fans who have followed both athletes for years, this link-up felt special and personal, not just another celebrity picture.Chloe Kim, 25, has been one of the most recognized faces in snowboarding since her teenage years. She won gold in the halfpipe at the 2018 PyeongChang Olympics and again at the 2022 Beijing Olympics.In Milan Cortina 2026, she added a silver medal to her collection. That brings her total to two gold medals and one silver.After the Games wrapped up, Kim spent time in snowy New York City.She was attending events and doing interviews when she crossed paths with Suni Lee.Sunisa “Suni” Lee is already a household name in gymnastics. She won the all-around gold medal at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, along with team silver and uneven bars bronze. Over her Olympic career, Lee has earned six medals, two gold, one silver, and three bronze.When Kim and Lee posed together, fans quickly noticed the power in that photo. Nine Olympic medals. Two different sports. One shared journey.Beyond medals, there is something deeper that connects them. Both proudly represent their heritage. Chloe Kim was born in the United States to South Korean immigrant parents.Suni Lee, born Sunisa Phabsomphou, is of Hmong descent, and her mother immigrated from Laos. Over the years, both athletes have spoken openly about what it means to represent their families and communities on the world stage.Seeing them together in New York felt like a full-circle moment. The competition may be over, but their impact continues. Instead of focusing on scores or rankings, this time it was about friendship, pride, and enjoying life after the Games. For fans, it was simple. Two champions, one city and a moment worth celebrating.
Article Summary: Chloe Kim and Sunisa "Suni" Lee share Olympic bond during New York meet-up. The two Olympic champions met in New York City and shared a photo together. The photo showed two of Team USA’s biggest stars smiling side by side.
